problems.statement: When I update the RSM and publish a new revision, I want confirmation from every mechanic in the system — a required read-and-acknowledge that they can't dismiss without reading the affected sections.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

problems.statement: REQ-GH-01: Hard Block on AD-Open Work Order Close Work orders with associated ADs that have no compliance record (or no documented inapplicability determination) must be blocked from closure.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: The distinction being: task card steps can queue, RTS cannot.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T04.

problems.statement: He cannot run a report across all PW535E LLPs in inventory sorted by remaining cycles.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

problems.statement: The cycle counter gap is a launch blocker for turbine LLP work — not a Phase 3 item; a turbine MRO system that cannot verify cycle status is not a turbine MRO system
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

problems.statement: Things I do in spreadsheets because the software can't do them, or can't do them in a way I trust.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T05.

problems.statement: Return-to-service must hard-block on any open discrepancy, unsigned card, or parts-traceability gap — no warning-only path
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

problems.statement: RTS authorization block on missing `form_337_ref` must produce a clear, specific error message — not a generic validation failure — naming the field and directing the tech to where it must be populated
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

problems.statement: I can't see what the current task is, I can't see parts status, I can't see notes without clicking into each job.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T10.

problems.statement: If every sign-off requires a specific, correct, verifiable approved data reference — document, revision, section — then the traceability problem mostly solves itself.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: Right now, I can't do that in any system I've ever used.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

problems.statement: I can't assess it at all if I don't know it was there.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

problems.statement: Within 48 hours of expiry, the discrepancy must appear as a priority alert in the QCM dashboard that cannot be dismissed without an action (extend with documentation, or mark resolved).
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T06.

problems.statement: Maintenance release must be system-blocked — not warned — until all work order steps are closed; any override must generate a logged audit entry
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: Any amendment to the association requires an explicit action with a documented reason, a separate audit log entry, and cannot be performed by the user who created the original entry without supervisor approval.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: Closure must be blocked until all items are green.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T06.

problems.statement: The completed IA entry must be immutable. He cannot sign off an annual and have the record be editable afterward without a documented amendment trail.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: If I can't explain it, I can't defend it.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: The part cannot be available to issue to a work order until the queue syncs and the server validates it.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T04.

problems.statement: It's not a maintenance item — it can't be inspected back to serviceable.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

problems.statement: You can't auto-generate a regulatory narrative.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

problems.statement: So you can't rely on the audit log being complete.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

problems.statement: Not an email I might miss — something in the dashboard that I can't ignore.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T06.

problems.statement: Submitting a maintenance record that fails any minimum-content check must be blocked with a specific error message identifying the missing element.
problems.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

features.statement: The work order should not be closeable if any AD compliance record is unresolved. Hard block.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T09.

features.statement: The signature action must be atomic and immediately confirmed. When he completes the authentication step, the system must display the completed record within two seconds.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: RTS sign-off screen must show complete summary: what was done, on what aircraft, on what date, by whom, against what reference — before authentication
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: Incoming parts receipt must require 8130-3 documentation before the part can be moved to "available" status
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T07.

features.statement: RTS authorization screen must display full documentation chain before sign is enabled: N-number, serial, WO#, repair description, repair classification (stated explicitly), 337 reference, 337 preparer name
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

features.statement: Manages FAA-approved revision process — every RSM change must go through her and receive DOM sign-off before distribution.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: Both should be in the record, separately, so an inspector can pull either one without reading a paragraph of my handwriting.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: The signature must be uniquely linked to the signer's certificate number, not their employee record, not their user account, not their email address.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

features.statement: The A&P or IA certificate number must appear in the signature record.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

features.statement: The signature must be timestamped to the action, not to the session.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: Certificate number (not username, not employee ID) must appear on every signature record in the live system and in all exports
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

features.statement: Exported / printed work order must be fully self-contained and inspector-readable without database access
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: Signature timestamps must reflect the time of the action, not the time of session start or sync
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

features.statement: Voiding or reversing a signed step must create a permanent, visible audit entry — original record must remain readable, not deleted
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: If an AD compliance action happened in work order number X, it should be bound to work order X for the life of the record.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: The signature should be an event, not a field.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T03.

features.statement: Error message must name the specific AD(s) and provide a direct action path to the AD compliance record.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: REQ-GH-02: Immutable AD-to-Work-Order Binding Once an AD compliance record is associated with a work order, that association must be permanent and audited.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: The system must be able to demonstrate, to an FAA inspector, that the person who signed was authenticated at the moment of signing.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: REQ-GH-05: Active-First Default Dashboard The work order dashboard must default to showing "Active" work orders on first load and on each morning session.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T10.

features.statement: Every AD compliance action must link immutably to the work order it was performed in. Not by reference, not by user entry — by the system.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: Nadia Solis should reference Rosa's validation explicitly.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T01.

features.statement: If someone changes a field after his signature, there must be an audit record, a re-signing requirement, and a notification to him.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

features.statement: He must be able to export a printed record at any time that looks like a traditional maintenance log entry — plain language, his identifying information, the work described, the signature indicator.
features.rationale: Observed in 1 source(s) with dominant linkage to T02.

timeline.event: Dispatch No. 23: The Version They Asked For

open_questions.question: How does that flow through your current process?
And how does Corridor fit into that reconciliation?
How do your techs work through a 100-hour today?
How do you feel about PIN-based re-authentication for signing?
What happens in the last hour before an aircraft goes back to an owner?
How do you want the system to handle that?
How do you manage discrepancy tracking today, and what breaks in the current workflow?
How do you manage QC review of closed work orders?
When a mechanic at Summit does a task, how does the approved data reference end up in the maintenance record?
What happens to those when an FAA inspector sees them?
How do you handle a situation where something on an aircraft turns out to be much more urgent?
